This is what we do.

We've been doing this for Adobe for 10 years plus.

The backstory of this company is that there was a cloud service provider, a managed service provider that was doing this for Adobe, was hosting Adobe's federal production environment for the last 10 years.
And then I bought that company in 2023 because I needed to get my last company FedRamped.
And it was the fastest way for me to get that company FedRamped, which is wild.
But that was literally the fastest way to do it.

And then after I got the company FedRamp in six months, and then just kind of kept building the business, and then passed forward two years, it was clear that it just was very valuable to be able to FedRamp things faster, but you needed a trusted landing zone to put it into, a condo building, if you will, that had already been built on Main Street 10 years ago, just serving really one tenant for a while, and now we're just stacking floors.

So all that to say, there's so much pent up demand, or has been at least, where so far we have not wasted any time on folks who are like, well, I don't know about Fed, it's like, if you don't know about FedRamp, like, don't waste my time.

Like, come back to me when you do know, because your options are still going to be, once you are convinced, your options are do it yourself, which is three years, three million bucks, if you can get a sponsor, which nine times out of
Out of 10, you can't.
Or work with me or work with Palantir.
And the difference between me and Palantir is Palantir is going to require you to containerize your entire application.
And when you deploy it, you're going to have like three knobs you can change.
And that's it.
And it's a very restrictive way to do it.
They're going to tell you you have to use this for EDR, this for logging, this for patching.
It's like change everything about yourself and then you can move into my house.
